visual_checks: Look for multi-step forms or processes where the user may need to re-enter information they already provided
Check checkout flows — is the billing address pre-populated from the shipping address with a 'Same as shipping' option?
Look for forms that ask for the same information in different sections (e.g., name in a contact section and again in a payment section)
Check if previously entered data is auto-populated when the user returns to a previous step
Look for account registration flows that ask the user to re-enter their email or other information
Check multi-page forms to see if information from page 1 needs to be re-entered on page 2
pass_conditions: Information previously entered by or provided to the user that is required to be entered again in the same process is either auto-populated or available for the user to select
Multi-step forms carry forward previously entered data so the user does not need to re-type it
Checkout flows offer a 'Same as shipping address' checkbox or auto-populate billing from shipping
When a user returns to a previous step, their previously entered data is preserved in the form fields
Confirmation pages pre-fill fields from previously entered data for review purposes
fail_conditions: Users must re-enter their name, email, address, or other personal information that they already provided earlier in the same process
A multi-step form clears previously entered data when the user navigates between steps
Shipping and billing address forms require full re-entry of the address even when they are the same
Re-entering a previous step of a form clears the data entered in that step, requiring the user to type it again
A confirmation step shows entered data but if the user goes back to edit, all other fields are cleared
na_conditions: The form is a single-step process with no repeated information requests
Information must be re-entered for security purposes (e.g., re-entering password for confirmation is exempt only if security requires it)
Previously entered information is no longer valid and must be provided fresh
The process does not ask for the same information more than once
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Multi-step form flow could not be completed to verify data persistence across steps
Cannot test data auto-population from static screenshots of a single step
The full form process requires authentication or prior data that was not available during testing
common_mistakes: Checkout flows that require full re-entry of billing address even when identical to shipping address
Multi-step application forms where going back to a previous step clears previously entered data
Confirmation pages that require the user to re-enter their email address to 'verify' it
Profile update forms that clear all fields when the user changes one field, requiring re-entry of all data
Survey or questionnaire forms that lose data when navigating between sections
Booking flows that ask for passenger details multiple times (once for the flight, again for the hotel)

examples.pass: A checkout form has a 'Billing address same as shipping' checkbox that, when checked, auto-fills all billing address fields from the shipping address entered on the previous page. The user can uncheck to enter a different billing address.
examples.fail: A multi-step insurance application asks for the applicant's full name and date of birth on Step 1 (Personal Info), then asks for the exact same name and date of birth again on Step 3 (Beneficiary Info) with no auto-population, forcing the user to retype identical information.
examples.partial: A hotel booking flow auto-populates the guest name from the account profile on the first page, but the payment page requires re-entry of the billing name and address even when they match the profile data already provided.

plain_meaning: Information the user already entered or provided earlier in the same process must not be required to be entered again manually. It must either be auto-filled or made selectable (from a list, a previous-value option, etc.). Exceptions: when re-entry is essential, when re-entry is essential to security, or when the earlier information is no longer valid.
scope_applies_to: Wizards that collect information across steps
Forms that ask for shipping and billing addresses in sequence
Any process that repeats a question already answered
Profile data already on file being requested again in a task
scope_does_not_apply_to: Single-step forms that do not repeat questions
Security-essential re-entry (for example confirming a password)
Essential re-entry required by the nature of the task (for example confirming an email)
Cases where the previous value has become invalid
New information not previously entered in the same process
borderline_calibration: Confirming a password or typing an email twice is the classic exempt case, PASS.
Only flag when the same information is clearly required to be retyped with no auto-fill and no selection mechanism.
Prefer Not Enough Evidence when multi-step flows were not captured end-to-end.
